open_questions: Verify Class A price, share count, book, and debt against a current shareholder report (not aggregator).
Obtain PP&E / land breakout and any appraisal-style marks before promoting overlay uplift.
Reconcile **287,996** filed Class A shares vs **~1.21M** implied for book math.
Vicki shopbot brief if OTC disclosure page remains empty.
Appraisal-style **$300+** book per share claims remain **falsifier ceiling** only without evidence.
thesis: Hanover Foods Corporation is a family-controlled U.S. food processor founded in 1924 and headquartered in Hanover, Pennsylvania. **[Fact]** The company cans and freezes vegetables, beans, soups, entrees, mushrooms, and related private-label products for retail and food-service customers under brands including Hanover, Myers, Bon Ton, and Aunt Kitty's. **[Fact]** As of the last SEC annual report (fiscal year ended May 30, 2004), the company operated **15 plants** across Pennsylvania, Maryland, Delaware, New Jersey, and Guatemala, with **2,205** employees. **[Fact]** Class A common stock (OTC Pink: HNFSA) is the publicly quoted line; Class B voting stock and related trusts remain controlled by the Warehime family. **[Fact]** The company deregistered from regular SEC reporting after a December 2004 odd-lot tender offer; no EDGAR filings appear after January 2005.
why_market_wrong: The market may treat HNFSA as an illiquid OTC stub with no story, missing a **book-discount land-and-plant sleeve** on a still-operating regional processor. **[Inference]** At an indicative **~$55** Class A quote versus aggregator-reported GAAP book of **~$87.62** per share, the stock trades near **0.63×** stated book equity. **[Inference]** Predictive attribute: **holdco / family-control discount** plus **market structure discount** (OTC Pink, no current SEC or OTC Markets financial PDFs indexed, thin volume). **[Inference]** Owned plants and land are likely carried at historical cost, so economic asset value may exceed GAAP book, but that uplift is **not** in our base case without filed marks. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math sums to **~$61.24/sh** base economic value versus **~$55** price, but book and debt remain aggregator **[Assumption]** inputs.
